message:
chore(beads): close graph and history redesign beads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,7 +55,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","title":"Rebuild the bvr TUI around the full FrankenTUI showcase contract before claiming parity","description":"Background:
The current TUI audit showed that `bvr` is still architecturally closer to a text-report renderer than to a flagship operator console. Most screens are composed from large multiline strings dropped into generic bordered paragraphs, while the repo documentation and parity ledger still imply that the TUI is largely complete. That mismatch is now itself a project risk.

Why this bead exists:
This epic is the canonical redesign track for turning the audit into executable work. The core requirement is not merely to make the Rust TUI prettier. It is to deliberately exploit the advanced capabilities already available in FrankenTUI/`ftui` so that `bvr` first recovers the workflow confidence and visual richness of legacy `bv`, and only then goes beyond it.

Non-negotiable design intent:
- Use every relevant high-end FrankenTUI capability that materially improves parity and operator effectiveness: global shell chrome, theme cycling, responsive layout tiers, pane geometry, hit-tested interaction regions, richer search affordances, semantic panels, stronger signal density, grapheme-safe text handling, terminal hyperlinks, and layout/visual diagnostics.
- Do not confuse a stable snapshot with a good product. The redesign must optimize for scanability, confidence, density, discoverability, and mode identity.
- Do not simplify away legacy workflows. Better-than-legacy improvements are welcome only after the legacy job-to-be-done is actually covered.

Scope of this epic:
1. Reset the TUI parity contract so the tracker and docs no longer overstate reality.
2. Build the missing shell, layout, visual, and interaction infrastructure required for a serious FrankenTUI implementation.
3. Rebuild the flagship main screen, then the board, graph, insights, and history surfaces around that infrastructure.
4. Add redesign-specific diagnostics and proof harnesses so future maintainers can validate quality without relying on tribal memory.

Acceptance:
- The child beads are sufficiently detailed that an implementer does not need to reopen `TUI_GAP_ANALYSIS.md` or prior planning docs to know what success means.
- The resulting implementation path is explicitly ordered: contract reset -> infrastructure -> flagship main screen -> secondary modes -> proof/evidence.
- Docs/verdict beads later in the tree depend on this redesign evidence instead of restating optimistic claims.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","parity","redesign","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","title":"Reset the TUI parity contract around FrankenTUI-first redesign and evidence-based claims","description":"Purpose:
Convert the audit into a tracker-native contract that future agents can trust without reopening the markdown report. This bead is the truth-reset step for the TUI program.

Background and reasoning:
The project currently says the TUI is effectively complete, but the audit found a more serious reality: the app is only lightly using FrankenTUI and still behaves like a string-rendered report viewer in many places. If we skip this contract-reset step, future work will keep optimizing the wrong target and closing beads against the wrong standard.

Required scope:
- Capture in the issue tracker that the redesign must leverage the full relevant FrankenTUI showcase contract, not just `Paragraph` + `Block` + ad hoc color tokens.
- Name the required capability families explicitly: global shell, theme/discoverability, responsive layout tiers, hit-tested regions, adjustable panes, semantic panels, search excellence, signal density, hyperlinks, grapheme-safe text metrics, and visual diagnostics.
- Define what "parity first, then exceed legacy" means for TUI work so teams do not chase novelty before workflow recovery.

Acceptance:
- The tracker itself contains the redesign rationale, the bar, and the list of non-negotiable capabilities.
- Future implementation beads can reference this bead as the canonical intent document instead of relying on external markdown.
- This bead does not close until the rest of the redesign subtree is coherent and dependency-complete.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Treat this bead as the canonical tracker-native contract for the redesign, not a prose-only summary.\n2. Make the capability families explicit enough that later beads can be audited against them without reopening markdown planning docs.\n3. Include the user-outcome bar: recover legacy workflow confidence and look/feel first, then exceed it with FrankenTUI-native improvements.\n4. Ensure the redesign subtree contains an explicit proof story, not just implementation stories.\n\nFuture-self note: If this contract is vague, later beads will optimize for whatever looks finished instead of what actually serves operators.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The tracker-native redesign contract explicitly names the capability families, user-experience bar, and parity-first-then-beyond sequencing.\n2. The subtree is coherent enough that future agents can work from beads alone without reopening TUI_GAP_ANALYSIS.md.\n3. The proof obligations for unit/state, snapshots, keyflows, and shell-level journeys are represented somewhere under this subtree.\n4. Docs and verdict beads later in the tree depend on this contract and the evidence package rather than optimistic narrative.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.282959098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T03:05:20.577160487Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T03:05:20.576729742Z","close_reason":"Completed tracker-backed parity-contract reset and aligned repo-facing docs with the FrankenTUI-first redesign/proof program","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","frankentui","parity","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.282959098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","title":"Rebuild history mode as an investigation surface with event rail, commit cards, file-focus lens, and context-preserving cross-mode jumps","description":"Purpose:
Turn history mode into a serious investigative workspace rather than a long text report with multiple sections.

Background:
History mode is one of the richer current screens, but it is still constrained by the same string-first rendering model as everything else. The result is that the operator can technically inspect many things, yet the workflow still feels approximate instead of polished.

Required scope:
- Improve the visual structure of timelines, milestones, commits, related beads, and file drilldowns.
- Strengthen file-tree and related-bead workflows so they behave like an investigation tool rather than an add-on.
- Make cross-mode jumps preserve context and reduce operator disorientation.

Acceptance:
- History mode feels like a coherent investigation surface.
- The relationship between timeline, commit detail, file focus, and related-bead context is much clearer.
- Cross-mode continuity is better than the current approximation.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. History mode should optimize for investigation rhythm: chronology, causality, related artifacts, and context-preserving jumps.\n2. Do not lose any current search, file-tree, or confidence capabilities while upgrading the surface.\n3. Use richer cards, rails, and panels so event and commit scanning stop feeling like long prose.\n\nFuture-self note: History is the place where operators ask what happened and why. If the rebuilt mode makes that harder, even with prettier visuals, it is a failure.","acceptance_criteria":"1. History mode becomes a coherent investigation workspace rather than a stacked report.\n2. Timeline, commit, file-tree, and jump workflows are all stronger than the current implementation without dropping functionality.\n3. Context preservation across jumps is explicitly tested.\n4. Unit/state tests, snapshots for multiple widths, and multi-step journey coverage prove the rebuilt history workflows.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:45.654940518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.930169698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","history","investigation","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:28.775813748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:45.654940518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:01.736153111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:04.984243525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:02.127388456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:05.395204762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:02.516003035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:05.812287900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:02.915659462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:03.328921171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","title":"Redesign history timelines, milestone/event composition, and commit cards for fast investigation and chronology scanning","description":"Purpose:
Improve the core visual grammar of history mode so chronology and event significance are visible without reading dense text blocks.

Required scope:
- Rework event/timeline composition into a clearer rail or card-based chronology.
- Make milestone and lifecycle state changes more visually legible.
- Rebuild commit presentation so commit cards and change summaries are easier to scan and compare.

Acceptance:
- Chronology is easier to understand at a glance.
- Commit/event reading is faster and less text-fatiguing.
- The screen has stronger investigation rhythm than the current report-like output.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. History should read like a chronology tool, not a visually upgraded transcript.\n2. Event, milestone, and commit cards must remain comparable under dense timelines and history-rich projects.\n3. Sparse history, partial history, and ambiguous chronology need intentional presentation so the user understands what is missing.\n4. Responsive fallbacks must preserve time ordering and event significance even when there is no room for the richest layout.\n\nFuture-self note: If users still have to read line by line to reconstruct what happened, this bead is not succeeding.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Chronology is easier to understand at a glance.\n2. Commit/event reading is faster and less text-fatiguing across dense and sparse history cases.\n3. Partial or thin history is communicated honestly without destroying investigation rhythm.\n4. Snapshot, keyflow, and scenario-driven proof cover dense timelines, sparse histories, and responsive chronology layouts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.023159910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:26:01.744593257Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","history","layout","timeline","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.023159910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:03.732591731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:04.184069676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Chronology easier to scan with visual differentiation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","history","layout","timeline","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.023159910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:03.732591731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:04.184069676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","title":"Upgrade history file-tree, related-bead drilldowns, link affordances, and context-preserving jumps","description":"Purpose:
Strengthen the interactive workflows that make history mode worth using during real investigations.

Required scope:
- Improve file-tree interaction, visibility, and focus signaling.
- Make related-bead drilldowns clearer and easier to traverse.
- Use terminal links or other first-class affordances where they improve navigation to commits, issues, repos, or docs.
- Preserve and improve context when jumping between history and adjacent modes.

Acceptance:
- File-tree and related-bead workflows feel deliberate rather than bolted on.
- Link and jump behavior reduces context loss instead of increasing it.
- History mode becomes a reliable place to investigate cause and sequence.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. File-tree and related-bead workflows should reduce investigation cost, not add another navigation puzzle.\n2. Preserve context aggressively when jumping out and back, especially after filtering, confidence changes, or file narrowing.\n3. Interactive elements must remain usable under long paths, Unicode, dense file sets, and sparse result sets.\n4. Make it obvious when history evidence is partial or when a jump is leaving the current investigative frame.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is about investigation continuity. If users keep losing their place when drilling around, the rebuilt history mode still is not trustworthy.","acceptance_criteria":"1. File-tree and related-bead workflows feel deliberate rather than bolted on.\n2. Link and jump behavior reduces context loss instead of increasing it, including after filters and confidence changes.\n3. Dense, sparse, long-path, and partial-evidence cases remain usable and honest.\n4. Unit/state, hit-region, and journey proof cover drilldowns, link handoffs, jump-back continuity, and partial-history edge cases with detailed logging.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.390188565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:26:08.224099101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["files","frankentui","history","links","navigation","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.390188565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:05.465037595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:04.594486365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:05.032449046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","title":"Add FrankenTUI-driven layout diagnostics and deterministic visual-debug tooling for the TUI redesign","description":"Purpose:
Make the redesign debuggable. Complex responsive TUI work should not rely on guesswork and snapshots alone.

Background:
The FrankenTUI showcase stack includes stronger layout-debug and deterministic-visual-debug patterns than `bvr` currently uses. The redesign will touch information architecture, widths, splitters, and hit regions across many screens, so the project needs better developer tools before it scales the redesign too far.

Required scope:
- Introduce or emulate layout-debugging aids that help explain why panels receive the sizes they do.
- Add deterministic visual-debug hooks suitable for testing complex screen states and responsive transitions.
- Make it easier to inspect hit regions, pane boundaries, or screen-mode thresholds while developing redesign work.

Acceptance:
- Developers have better-than-current tools for diagnosing wasted space, clipping, and awkward screen composition.
- The redesign is no longer validated only by staring at final snapshots.
- The tooling is practical enough to be reused across multiple screen rebuilds.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Prefer reusable diagnostics that multiple redesign beads can share over one-off debug hacks.\n2. Focus on layout and constraint visibility, hit-region visibility, and deterministic visual-state capture.\n3. Tooling should make developers faster during redesign, not become a side project detached from the product.\n\nFuture-self note: Without this bead, later TUI work will still be debugged by intuition and screenshot diffing. That is too weak for the scale of redesign being planned here.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The TUI redesign has practical diagnostics for layout, pane, or hit-region reasoning.\n2. Deterministic debug capture exists for non-trivial screen states and responsive transitions.\n3. The tooling is documented enough to be reused by later maintainers.\n4. Tests cover the diagnostics where feasible so the tools themselves do not rot.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.772906382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:01:19.179035891Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:01:19.178546725Z","close_reason":"Both children closed: 3.6.11.1 (layout/hit-region diagnostics) and 3.6.11.2 (debug capture/replay hooks). Implemented by SageSpring.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","frankentui","layout","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.772906382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:59.452550804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:59.852160600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:05.891553480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.1","title":"Build layout and hit-region diagnostics for redesign work: pane maps, constraint visibility, and interaction overlays","description":"Purpose:
Create reusable diagnostics that expose where panes, constraints, and hit regions actually are during redesign work.

Required scope:
1. Make pane boundaries and hit regions inspectable enough to debug shell and screen interaction bugs.
2. Provide a practical way to inspect layout allocation and identify wasted or clipped space.
3. Keep the diagnostics lightweight enough that they can be used repeatedly during implementation.

Acceptance:
1. Developers can inspect layout and hit-region problems without guessing from screenshots alone.
2. The diagnostics are reusable across multiple redesigned screens.
3. Tests or deterministic checks cover the core diagnostic logic where feasible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.421620873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T17:06:46.859481055Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T17:06:46.858893102Z","close_reason":"Added reusable layout and hit-region debug render modes in src/tui.rs with deterministic tests across main/history/graph views; corrected wide-history breakpoint expectations.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","frankentui","hittest","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.421620873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -88,7 +88,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","title":"Add lane summary modules and richer board cards with density tuned for narrow, medium, and wide layouts","description":"Purpose:
Upgrade the visual grammar of the board itself so lanes and cards carry useful information before the detail pane is involved.

Required scope:
- Add lane summary treatments that can surface count, blocked share, stale share, or other lane-health signals rather than only raw totals.
- Rebuild card previews so they communicate status, priority, title, and at least one additional meaningful signal without requiring a detail jump.
- Create width-aware variants so the board can show more useful content instead of large empty gutters.

Acceptance:
- Lanes have stronger visual and informational identity.
- Cards feel like cards, not just indented text lines.
- The board stays useful across widths.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Lanes must communicate pressure, health, and comparative meaning at a glance, not just counts.\n2. Cards should preserve spatial memory and still work when titles, labels, or metadata are long, sparse, or numerous.\n3. Empty and nearly empty lanes need intentional treatments so the board still feels informative instead of hollow.\n4. Use width-aware card/lane variants that preserve usefulness under crowding rather than simply shrinking text.\n\nFuture-self note: If the board still has large empty gutters or cards that only differ by title text, this bead is underdone.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Lanes have stronger visual and informational identity, including meaningful empty-lane and low-density treatments.\n2. Cards feel like cards, not just indented text lines, even with long or messy metadata.\n3. The board stays useful across widths, crowding levels, and sparse/fallback states.\n4. Snapshot and keyflow proof cover lane/card density behavior, empty-lane presentation, and crowded-board layouts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.498365357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T06:20:19.019488079Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T06:20:19.019009293Z","close_reason":"Lane summaries upgraded: health indicator (blocked/clear/empty), type+status icons on cards, blocker/unblocks indicators (⊘N/↓N), priority badges, 8-card preview limit, descriptive empty states.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","density","frankentui","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:54.637445259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.006832223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.498365357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","title":"Add board hit-testing, pane behavior, and navigation/discoverability parity for a true workspace feel","description":"Purpose:
Make board mode interact like a first-class workspace rather than a passive grouped list.

Required scope:
- Add hit-tested selection and focus movement for lane and card interaction.
- Improve detail split behavior and pane allocation so the operator can actually work from the board instead of just glancing at it.
- Make search, lane jumps, and focus movement more discoverable and less dependent on memorized footer prose.
- Ensure the board uses the shared shell conventions rather than inventing another control language.

Acceptance:
- The board has strong keyboard and mouse interaction paths.
- Focus and navigation are clearer than in the current implementation.
- The board/detail relationship feels intentional and ergonomic.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Make board interactions fast enough that an operator could plausibly stay in board mode to work, not just glance at it.\n2. Lane jumps, focus changes, and detail split behavior must remain understandable under mouse and keyboard use, including after resizing or filtering.\n3. Treat no-hit, single-lane, and sparse-board states as real operator flows with clear recovery cues.\n4. Keep board-specific discoverability near the relevant lanes/cards rather than outsourcing everything to global help.\n\nFuture-self note: If the board still feels like a grouped report after interaction starts, this bead has not landed.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The board has strong keyboard and mouse interaction paths.\n2. Focus and navigation are clearer than in the current implementation, including after resize, filter, and no-hit transitions.\n3. The board/detail relationship feels intentional and ergonomic in both dense and sparse states.\n4. Unit/state, hit-region, and journey proof cover lane jumps, split behavior, focus recovery, and board-specific recovery flows with verbose diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.847108314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:07.994392240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","discoverability","frankentui","interaction","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.385950129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.779058570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.847108314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:56.171426497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","title":"Rebuild graph mode as a topology exploration surface instead of a node profile report","description":"Purpose:
Turn graph mode into a mode where users actually understand structure, bottlenecks, and neighborhoods instead of reading a long metric profile for one node at a time.

Background:
Current graph mode is analytically useful, but experientially it is still a ranked list plus an ASCII ego box and a long metric section. That is not enough to create graph intuition or make the mode feel indispensable.

Required scope:
- Reframe graph mode around neighborhood exploration and relationship navigation.
- Improve the presentation of graph metrics so they aid interpretation without dominating the screen.
- Add stronger cross-links and drill paths so graph mode can hand off effectively to detail/history/other views.

Acceptance:
- Users can perceive graph structure faster and more confidently than in the current implementation.
- The mode feels graph-native rather than report-native.
- Metric presentation supports topology understanding instead of burying it.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Graph mode should prioritize topology comprehension and traversal confidence over static metric dumping.\n2. The rebuilt graph should use badges, panels, and links that support action, not just observation.\n3. Preserve the analytically valuable metrics, but present them in a way that earns their screen real estate.\n\nFuture-self note: This mode is where  proves it is more than a task list. If the operator cannot feel graph structure quickly, the redesign missed the point.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Graph mode materially improves neighborhood comprehension and traversal.\n2. Metric presentation is denser and more actionable than the current long report format.\n3. Cross-links to adjacent workflows exist where they improve operator flow.\n4. Deterministic tests and journey/snapshot proof cover topology-focused interactions and width-tier behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.206885414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.903466094Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","graph","topology","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:28.065882333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.206885414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:56.586153120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:03.180462475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:56.950941730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:03.594684496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:57.343846863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:04.015781545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:57.775337062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","title":"Redesign graph neighborhood visualization and relationship traversal around blockers, dependents, cycles, and focal context","description":"Purpose:
Give graph mode a stronger neighborhood metaphor so the operator can reason about topology, not just metrics.

Required scope:
- Rework the focal-node presentation so blockers, dependents, and local structure are easier to parse than the current mostly textual composition.
- Improve traversal affordances for moving through relationships and local neighborhoods.
- Surface cycle membership and local criticality in a way that helps graph understanding instead of hiding it in later text sections.

Acceptance:
- Neighborhood structure is materially easier to understand than in the current graph screen.
- Relationship traversal feels intentional and supports exploration.
- The focal graph area earns the name "graph mode".","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Prioritize topology comprehension over ornament: blockers, dependents, cycles, and focal context should be faster to read than in the current graph.\n2. Traversal must remain usable under dense local neighborhoods, sparse neighborhoods, and cycle-heavy cases.\n3. The focal presentation should make it clear what changed as the user traverses, so exploration does not become disorienting.\n4. Responsive fallbacks must preserve relationship meaning even when there is not enough room for the richest composition.\n\nFuture-self note: If graph exploration still feels mentally expensive or easy to get lost in, this bead is not done.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Neighborhood structure is materially easier to understand than in the current graph screen.\n2. Relationship traversal feels intentional and supports exploration across dense, sparse, and cycle-heavy local neighborhoods.\n3. Responsive and degraded layouts still preserve the core relationship story rather than collapsing into ambiguous text.\n4. Unit/state, snapshot, and journey proof cover traversal, focal-context updates, and dense-neighborhood edge cases.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.566060523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:16.529383305Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","graph","layout","relationships","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:58.167971592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:58.566932927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.566060523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.2","title":"Rebuild graph metrics, badges, and cross-links so graph mode supports action instead of static inspection","description":"Purpose:
Make graph mode actionable by improving metric framing, badge semantics, and links to adjacent workflows.

Required scope:
- Replace the long vertical metric report with a denser and more modular metric cockpit.
- Use stronger badges and micro-panels for rank, risk, degree, cut-point/cycle state, and other high-signal properties.
- Add actionable cross-links and terminal-native link affordances where useful so graph mode can hand off to issue detail, history, docs, or external references.

Acceptance:
- Metric scanning is faster and less monotonous than in the current implementation.
- The screen makes it easier to decide what to inspect next.
- Graph mode links effectively into the rest of the product.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Metrics must help the user decide where to go next, not merely prove the analyzer is sophisticated.\n2. Cross-links should reduce friction into detail, history, and docs flows without turning graph mode into a link dump.\n3. Preserve interpretability when metric data is sparse, approximate, or visually compressed by narrow layouts.\n4. Make badge semantics stable enough that users can learn them across screens.\n\nFuture-self note: If users still admire metrics without acting on them, this bead did not translate graph analytics into workflow value.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Metric scanning is faster and less monotonous than in the current implementation.\n2. The screen makes it easier to decide what to inspect next, including when metric coverage is partial or compressed.\n3. Graph mode links effectively into the rest of the product without losing focus clarity.\n4. Unit, snapshot, and journey/keyflow proof cover metric interpretation surfaces, cross-link handoffs, and narrow-layout degradation with detailed logs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.917266775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:42.300983928Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","graph","links","metrics","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:58.963509414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:59.353509481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:44.917266775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:59.762339169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","title":"Rebuild insights mode as a responsive analytics console with stronger visual identity and outlier surfacing","description":"Purpose:
Turn insights mode into a genuine analysis console rather than a sequence of textual analytics panels.

Background:
The current insights mode has useful computations, but the mode identity is weak because the information is still presented mostly as text. That undercuts the sense that this view is where a user comes to understand the system, spot anomalies, and decide where to dig deeper.

Required scope:
- Use tiles, metric clusters, heatmaps, ranked outlier modules, and stronger section framing rather than relying on one long textual panel body.
- Make responsive visibility decisions so the most valuable insight modules survive on smaller terminals.
- Preserve explanation/proof affordances while upgrading the visual analytics layer.

Acceptance:
- Insights mode has a clear analytics-console identity.
- High-signal findings are easier to spot at a glance.
- The mode balances explainability with visual density.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Insights mode must become visually distinct from all report-like surfaces.\n2. Keep explanation and proof affordances, but organize them under a stronger analytics-console IA.\n3. Use responsive visibility rules so high-value insight modules remain visible when space is tight.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is where the product starts to feel like an advanced triage engine rather than just a viewer with extra screens.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Insights mode has a clear analytics-console identity with stronger outlier surfacing and visual hierarchy.\n2. Explanation and proof behavior still exists and remains testable.\n3. Width-aware layouts preserve the most valuable insight modules instead of degrading randomly.\n4. Unit/state tests plus snapshots and journeys cover panel switching, explainability toggles, and responsive behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:45.280277520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T20:29:28.818458796Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","frankentui","insights","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:28.401976705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:45.280277520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:00.160407641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:00.547578337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:00.941308840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:04.417426272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:01.335539330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:58.636112269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:59.047321979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","author":"MagentaCove","text":"Starting a concrete implementation slice under the insights redesign epic. br refused direct claims on child beads bd-2bpl.3.6.9.1 / .9.2 because they are blocked by the parent-child dependency, so I am working the parent epic with an immediate coding focus on the first-glance analytics surface in src/tui.rs: replacing more of the rotating report feel with denser outlier/tile-style summary blocks and adding focused proof if needed. This keeps tracker state truthful while avoiding overlap with current src/main.rs path/loader work.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:24:02Z"},{"id":156,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","author":"MagentaCove","text":"Implementation slice in progress in src/tui.rs: insights list pane now opens with signal tiles, outlier radar, and panel-focus context; insights detail pane now starts with an analytics cockpit and compact metric strip before the full evidence dump. Verification so far via rch: cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo test --lib insights_mode_renders_rank_sections passed; cargo test --lib insights_detail_shows_all_metrics_for_focused_issue passed. Narrow insights snapshot currently fails only because the expected first-screen contract changed in the touched area, not because of a crash or unrelated render breakage.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:28:41Z"},{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","author":"MagentaCove","text":"Verified narrow insights redesign slice landed in src/tui.rs. Changes: list pane now opens with Signal Tiles, Outlier Radar, and Panel Focus context before the per-panel ranked rows; detail pane now opens with Analytics Cockpit and a compact Metric Strip before the longer evidence dump. Proof: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib insights_mode_renders_rank_sections; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib insights_detail_shows_all_metrics_for_focused_issue; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib snap_insights_narrow. This is a concrete first-glance density improvement under the parent insights redesign epic while the child beads remain blocked by the tracker’s parent-child dependency model.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:29:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.1","title":"Rebuild insights mode around analytic tiles, outlier surfacing, and stronger at-a-glance signal density","description":"Purpose:
Make insights mode visually and operationally distinct by turning it into an analytics surface instead of a rotating text report.

Required scope:
1. Introduce tile/grid-style analytic modules where appropriate.
2. Surface outliers, bottlenecks, and unusual patterns more aggressively than the current mode does.
3. Use stronger metric visuals and section hierarchy so high-value signals are obvious quickly.

Acceptance:
1. The first glance at insights mode communicates analysis, not prose.
2. Operators can identify notable patterns faster than in the current implementation.
3. Snapshot/journey evidence proves the new density and hierarchy across widths.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. The first screenful must reveal the important pattern or anomaly before the user starts reading explanatory prose.\n2. Tile and grid density should remain meaningful under wide, medium, and narrow layouts instead of degenerating into unlabeled clutter.\n3. Empty, sparse, or low-signal analytical states need intentional presentation so the mode stays trustworthy even when there is little to show.\n4. Stronger density must not hide provenance; important tiles should still signal where the number came from or where to drill next.\n\nFuture-self note: If insights only looks busier rather than more revealing, the redesign is superficial.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The first glance at insights mode communicates analysis, not prose.\n2. Operators can identify notable patterns faster than in the current implementation, including under sparse and low-signal datasets.\n3. Snapshot, journey, and scenario-driven proof demonstrate density and hierarchy across widths, including empty/fallback analytical states.\n4. High-value tiles remain interpretable enough to support drilldown rather than becoming ornamental numbers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.596462923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:49.782071183Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","density","frankentui","insights","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.596462923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
